papers.title: OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking
papers.abstract: Abstract OpenClaw-RL framework enables policy learning from diverse next-state signals across multiple interaction modalities using asynchronous training with PRM judges and hindsight-guided distillation.  					AI-generated summary Every agent interaction generates a next-state signal, namely the user reply, tool output, terminal or GUI state change that follows each action, yet no existing agentic RL system recovers it as a live, online learning source. We present OpenClaw-RL, a framework built on a simple observation: next-state signals are universal, and policy can learn from all of them simultaneously. Personal conversations, terminal executions, GUI interactions, SWE tasks, and tool-call traces are not separate training problems. They are all interactions that can be used to train the same policy in the same loop. Next-state signals encode two forms of information: evaluative signals, which indicate how well the action performed and are extracted as scalar rewards via a PRM judge; and directive signals, which indicate how the action should have been different and are recovered through Hindsight-Guided On-Policy Distillation (OPD). We extract textual hints from the next state, construct an enhanced teacher context, and provide token-level directional advantage supervision that is richer than any scalar reward. Due to the asynchronous design, the model serves live requests, the PRM judges ongoing interactions, and the trainer updates the policy at the same time, with zero coordination overhead between them. Applied to personal agents, OpenClaw-RL enables an agent to improve simply by being used, recovering conversational signals from user re-queries, corrections, and explicit feedback. Applied to general agents, the same infrastructure supports scalable RL across terminal, GUI, SWE, and tool-call settings, where we additionally demonstrate the utility of process rewards. Code: https://github.com/Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL

ru.title: Универсальное обучение агента из сигналов следующего состояния
ru.desc: OpenClaw-RL — это фреймворк для обучения политики агента с подкреплением, который использует сигналы о следующих состояниях из различных типов взаимодействий: диалоги, терминальные команды, GUI и инструменты. Система извлекает два типа информации из этих сигналов: оценочные сигналы (через PRM судей для скалярных наград) и директивные сигналы (через метод Hindsight-Guided On-Policy Distillation для более богатого обучения на уровне токенов). Асинхронный дизайн позволяет модели одновременно обслуживать пользовательские запросы, оценивать взаимодействия и обновлять политику без координационных издержек. Этот подход позволяет личным агентам улучшаться во время использования, извлекая полезную информацию из переформулировок, исправлений пользователя и явной обратной связи.

en.title: Learn from Every Interaction: OpenClaw-RL Unifies Policy Learning Across Modalities
en.desc: OpenClaw-RL is a framework that allows reinforcement learning (RL) agents to learn from various next-state signals generated during interactions, such as user replies and tool outputs. It utilizes asynchronous training with PRM judges to evaluate actions and Hindsight-Guided On-Policy Distillation (OPD) to refine policies based on what could have been done differently. This approach treats different interaction modalities, like personal conversations and GUI tasks, as interconnected learning opportunities rather than separate problems. By leveraging these diverse signals, OpenClaw-RL enables agents to improve continuously through real-time feedback without the need for coordination between components.

zh.title: OpenClaw-RL：多模态信号下的策略学习新框架
zh.desc: OpenClaw-RL框架通过异步训练和PRM评判者，利用多种交互模式中的多样化下一个状态信号进行策略学习。每个代理交互都会生成下一个状态信号，这些信号可以作为在线学习的来源。该框架表明，下一个状态信号是普遍的，策略可以同时从所有信号中学习。通过提取文本提示和提供更丰富的方向性监督，OpenClaw-RL使得个人代理和通用代理能够在实际使用中不断改进。

papers.title: Flash-KMeans: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact K-Means
papers.abstract: Abstract Flash-kmeans enables efficient online k-means clustering on GPUs through novel kernel-level optimizations that eliminate I/O bottlenecks and atomic write contention.  					AI-generated summary k-means has historically been positioned primarily as an offline processing primitive, typically used for dataset organization or embedding preprocessing rather than as a first-class component in online systems. In this work, we revisit this classical algorithm under the lens of modern AI system design and enable k-means as an online primitive. We point out that existing GPU implementations of k-means remain fundamentally bottlenecked by low-level system constraints rather than theoretical algorithmic complexity. Specifically, the assignment stage suffers from a severe IO bottleneck due to the massive explicit materialization of the N times K distance matrix in High Bandwidth Memory (HBM). Simultaneously, the centroid update stage is heavily penalized by hardware-level atomic write contention caused by irregular, scatter-style token aggregations. To bridge this performance gap, we propose flash-kmeans, an IO-aware and contention-free k-means implementation for modern GPU workloads. Flash-kmeans introduces two core kernel-level innovations: (1) FlashAssign, which fuses distance computation with an online argmin to completely bypass intermediate memory materialization; (2) sort-inverse update, which explicitly constructs an inverse mapping to transform high-contention atomic scatters into high-bandwidth, segment-level localized reductions. Furthermore, we integrate algorithm-system co-designs, including chunked-stream overlap and cache-aware compile heuristics, to ensure practical deployability. Extensive evaluations on NVIDIA H200 GPUs demonstrate that flash-kmeans achieves up to 17.9times end-to-end speedup over best baselines, while outperforming industry-standard libraries like cuML and FAISS by 33times and over 200times, respectively.
affiliations: The University of Texas at Austin

ru.title: K-means в реальном времени: победа над системными ограничениями GPU
ru.desc: Flash-kmeans — это оптимизированная реализация классического алгоритма k-means кластеризации для GPU, которая преодолевает системные узкие места вместо алгоритмических. Авторы устранили две основные проблемы: избыточное расходование памяти при вычислении матрицы расстояний N×K и конфликты при одновременной записи при обновлении центроидов. Решение использует два ключевых приёма на уровне ядер GPU: слияние вычисления расстояний с поиском минимума для экономии памяти и сортировку с обратным отображением для преобразования конкурентных операций в локализованные редукции. Экспериментальные результаты показывают ускорение в 17.9 раз над базовыми методами и превосходство над промышленными библиотеками cuML и FAISS.

en.title: Revolutionizing Online K-means Clustering with Flash-kmeans
en.desc: This paper presents Flash-kmeans, a new approach to k-means clustering optimized for online processing on GPUs. Traditional k-means is often used for offline tasks, but this work adapts it for real-time applications by addressing performance bottlenecks in existing GPU implementations. The authors introduce two key innovations: FlashAssign, which eliminates the need for large distance matrices, and sort-inverse update, which reduces contention during centroid updates. Extensive testing shows that Flash-kmeans significantly outperforms existing libraries, achieving up to 17.9 times faster processing speeds.

zh.title: Flash-kmeans：高效在线聚类的新选择
zh.desc: Flash-kmeans 是一种高效的在线 k-means 聚类算法，专为 GPU 优化而设计。它通过消除 I/O 瓶颈和原子写入争用，解决了传统 k-means 在现代 AI 系统中的应用限制。该算法引入了两个核心创新：FlashAssign 和反向排序更新，显著提高了计算效率。实验结果表明，Flash-kmeans 在 NVIDIA H200 GPU 上的性能比现有最佳基线快 17.9 倍，且在速度上超越了行业标准库 cuML 和 FAISS。

papers.title: MA-EgoQA: Question Answering over Egocentric Videos from Multiple Embodied Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-agent systems require understanding multiple long-horizon egocentric videos simultaneously, necessitating new benchmarks and models for system-level comprehension.  					AI-generated summary As embodied models become powerful, humans will collaborate with multiple embodied AI agents at their workplace or home in the future. To ensure better communication between human users and the multi-agent system, it is crucial to interpret incoming information from agents in parallel and refer to the appropriate context for each query. Existing challenges include effectively compressing and communicating high volumes of individual sensory inputs in the form of video and correctly aggregating multiple egocentric videos to construct system-level memory. In this work, we first formally define a novel problem of understanding multiple long-horizon egocentric videos simultaneously collected from embodied agents. To facilitate research in this direction, we introduce MultiAgent-EgoQA (MA-EgoQA), a benchmark designed to systemically evaluate existing models in our scenario. MA-EgoQA provides 1.7k questions unique to multiple egocentric streams, spanning five categories: social interaction, task coordination, theory-of-mind, temporal reasoning, and environmental interaction. We further propose a simple baseline model for MA-EgoQA named EgoMAS, which leverages shared memory across embodied agents and agent-wise dynamic retrieval. Through comprehensive evaluation across diverse baselines and EgoMAS on MA-EgoQA, we find that current approaches are unable to effectively handle multiple egocentric streams, highlighting the need for future advances in system-level understanding across the agents. The code and benchmark are available at https://ma-egoqa.github.io.

ru.title: Системное понимание множества эгоцентрических видео в мультиагентных системах
ru.desc: Работа посвящена проблеме одновременного анализа нескольких длинных эгоцентрических видео от множества воплощённых AI агентов. Авторы предлагают новый бенчмарк MultiAgent-EgoQA (MA-EgoQA), содержащий 1.7k вопросов по пяти категориям: социальное взаимодействие, координация задач, теория сознания, временное рассуждение и взаимодействие с окружением. Для решения задачи предложена базовая модель EgoMAS, которая использует общую память между агентами и динамическую выборку информации для каждого агента. Исследование показывает, что существующие подходы неэффективны при обработке нескольких эгоцентрических потоков, что указывает на необходимость развития методов системного понимания мультиагентных сценариев.

en.title: Enhancing Multi-Agent Understanding Through Video Comprehension
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge of understanding multiple long-horizon egocentric videos from various embodied agents in multi-agent systems. It introduces a new benchmark called MultiAgent-EgoQA (MA-EgoQA) to evaluate models on their ability to process and comprehend these video streams. The benchmark includes 1.7k unique questions across five categories, focusing on social interactions and task coordination among agents. The authors also propose a baseline model, EgoMAS, which utilizes shared memory and dynamic retrieval to improve system-level comprehension, revealing that current methods struggle with this complex task.

zh.title: 多智能体系统的自我中心视频理解新挑战
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了多智能体系统如何同时理解多个长时间的自我中心视频。我们提出了一个新的基准MultiAgent-EgoQA（MA-EgoQA），用于评估现有模型在这一领域的表现。MA-EgoQA包含1700个独特问题，涵盖社交互动、任务协调、心智理论、时间推理和环境互动等五个类别。我们还提出了一个基线模型EgoMAS，利用智能体之间的共享记忆和动态检索，旨在提高对多自我中心视频流的理解能力。

papers.title: LLM2Vec-Gen: Generative Embeddings from Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract LLM2Vec-Gen introduces a self-supervised method for text embedding that represents model responses through trainable special tokens, achieving superior performance on MTEB while reducing harmful content and improving reasoning capabilities.  					AI-generated summary LLM-based text embedders typically encode the semantic content of their input. However, embedding tasks require mapping diverse inputs to similar outputs. Typically, this input-output is addressed by training embedding models with paired data using contrastive learning. In this work, we propose a novel self-supervised approach, LLM2Vec-Gen, which adopts a different paradigm: rather than encoding the input, we learn to represent the model's potential response. Specifically, we add trainable special tokens to the LLM's vocabulary, append them to input, and optimize them to represent the LLM's response in a fixed-length sequence. Training is guided by the LLM's own completion for the query, along with an unsupervised embedding teacher that provides distillation targets. This formulation helps to bridge the input-output gap and transfers LLM capabilities such as safety alignment and reasoning to embedding tasks. Crucially, the LLM backbone remains frozen and training requires only unlabeled queries. LLM2Vec-Gen achieves state-of-the-art self-supervised performance on the Massive Text Embedding Benchmark (MTEB), improving by 9.3% over the best unsupervised embedding teacher. We also observe up to 43.2% reduction in harmful content retrieval and 29.3% improvement in reasoning capabilities for embedding tasks. Finally, the learned embeddings are interpretable and can be decoded into text to reveal their semantic content.

ru.title: Обучение эмбеддингов через ответы модели вместо входных данных
ru.desc: LLM2Vec-Gen предлагает самоконтролируемый метод для создания текстовых эмбеддингов, который использует обучаемые специальные токены для представления потенциальных ответов языковой модели. Метод не требует размеченных данных и сохраняет замороженной основную модель, обучаясь только на немеченых запросах с помощью самих дополнений LLM и дистилляции от учителя эмбеддингов. Подход достигает современных результатов на бенчмарке MTEB с улучшением на 9.3% и успешно передает способности языковой модели, такие как выравнивание по безопасности и рассуждение, на задачи эмбеддинга. Полученные эмбеддинги интерпретируемы и могут быть декодированы обратно в текст для выявления их семантического содержания.

en.title: Revolutionizing Text Embeddings with LLM2Vec-Gen
en.desc: LLM2Vec-Gen is a self-supervised method for creating text embeddings that focuses on representing the potential responses of a language model rather than just encoding the input text. It introduces trainable special tokens that are optimized to capture the model's responses, allowing for a more effective mapping of diverse inputs to similar outputs. This approach leverages the model's own completions and an unsupervised embedding teacher to guide training, resulting in improved performance on the Massive Text Embedding Benchmark (MTEB). Additionally, LLM2Vec-Gen reduces harmful content retrieval and enhances reasoning capabilities, while maintaining interpretability of the learned embeddings.

zh.title: 自监督文本嵌入的新方法：LLM2Vec-Gen
zh.desc: LLM2Vec-Gen是一种自监督的文本嵌入方法，通过可训练的特殊标记来表示模型的响应。这种方法不同于传统的输入编码，而是学习如何表示模型的潜在响应。通过将可训练的特殊标记添加到输入中，并优化它们以表示LLM的响应，LLM2Vec-Gen在大规模文本嵌入基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。该方法还显著减少了有害内容的检索，并提高了推理能力。

papers.title: ReMix: Reinforcement routing for mixtures of LoRAs in LLM finetuning
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers address imbalance in routing weights of Mixture-of-LoRAs models by proposing Reinforcement Routing (ReMix), which uses non-learnable weights and reinforcement learning techniques to improve model expressiveness and performance.  					AI-generated summary Low-rank adapters (LoRAs) are a parameter-efficient finetuning technique that injects trainable low-rank matrices into pretrained models to adapt them to new tasks. Mixture-of-LoRAs models expand neural networks efficiently by routing each layer input to a small subset of specialized LoRAs of the layer. Existing Mixture-of-LoRAs routers assign a learned routing weight to each LoRA to enable end-to-end training of the router. Despite their empirical promise, we observe that the routing weights are typically extremely imbalanced across LoRAs in practice, where only one or two LoRAs often dominate the routing weights. This essentially limits the number of effective LoRAs and thus severely hinders the expressive power of existing Mixture-of-LoRAs models. In this work, we attribute this weakness to the nature of learnable routing weights and rethink the fundamental design of the router. To address this critical issue, we propose a new router designed that we call Reinforcement Routing for Mixture-of-LoRAs (ReMix). Our key idea is using non-learnable routing weights to ensure all active LoRAs to be equally effective, with no LoRA dominating the routing weights. However, our routers cannot be trained directly via gradient descent due to our non-learnable routing weights. Hence, we further propose an unbiased gradient estimator for the router by employing the reinforce leave-one-out (RLOO) technique, where we regard the supervision loss as the reward and the router as the policy in reinforcement learning. Our gradient estimator also enables to scale up training compute to boost the predictive performance of our ReMix. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed ReMix significantly outperform state-of-the-art parameter-efficient finetuning methods under a comparable number of activated parameters.

ru.title: Справедливая маршрутизация через обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый подход ReMix для маршрутизации в моделях Mixture-of-LoRAs, который решает проблему дисбаланса в распределении весов маршрутизации. Авторы заменили обучаемые веса маршрутизации на фиксированные, обеспечивая равное использование всех адаптеров LoRA. Для обучения такого маршрутизатора применены техники обучения с подкреплением, включая несмещённую оценку градиентов через метод RLOO. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что ReMix значительно превосходит существующие методы параметрически-эффективной адаптации моделей.

en.title: Equalizing LoRA Impact with Reinforcement Routing
en.desc: This paper introduces Reinforcement Routing (ReMix), a novel approach to improve the routing weights in Mixture-of-LoRAs models. The authors identify that traditional learned routing weights often lead to imbalances, where only a few LoRAs dominate, limiting the model's expressiveness. ReMix employs non-learnable routing weights to ensure that all LoRAs contribute equally, enhancing the model's performance. Additionally, the paper presents an unbiased gradient estimator using reinforcement learning techniques to optimize the routing process without direct gradient descent.

zh.title: ReMix：平衡Mixture-of-LoRAs模型的路由权重
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种名为ReMix的强化路由方法，以解决Mixture-of-LoRAs模型中路由权重不平衡的问题。ReMix使用非可学习的权重和强化学习技术，确保所有活跃的LoRA都能有效发挥作用，避免某个LoRA主导路由权重。通过引入无偏梯度估计器，ReMix能够在不直接使用梯度下降的情况下进行训练，从而提升模型的表现力和性能。实验结果表明，ReMix在参数效率微调方法中显著优于现有的最先进技术。

papers.title: In-Context Reinforcement Learning for Tool Use in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract In-Context Reinforcement Learning (ICRL) enables large language models to effectively use external tools through a reinforcement learning-only framework that eliminates the need for costly supervised fine-tuning by gradually reducing in-context examples during training.  					AI-generated summary While large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong reasoning abilities, their performance on complex tasks is often constrained by the limitations of their internal knowledge. A compelling approach to overcome this challenge is to augment these models with external tools -- such as Python interpreters for mathematical computations or search engines for retrieving factual information. However, enabling models to use these tools effectively remains a significant challenge. Existing methods typically rely on cold-start pipelines that begin with supervised fine-tuning (SFT), followed by reinforcement learning (RL). These approaches often require substantial amounts of labeled data for SFT, which is expensive to annotate or synthesize. In this work, we propose In-Context Reinforcement Learning (ICRL), an RL-only framework that eliminates the need for SFT by leveraging few-shot prompting during the rollout stage of RL. Specifically, ICRL introduces in-context examples within the rollout prompts to teach the model how to invoke external tools. Furthermore, as training progresses, the number of in-context examples is gradually reduced, eventually reaching a zero-shot setting where the model learns to call tools independently. We conduct extensive experiments across a range of reasoning and tool-use benchmarks. Results show that ICRL achieves state-of-the-art performance, demonstrating its effectiveness as a scalable, data-efficient alternative to traditional SFT-based pipelines.

ru.title: Обучение с подкреплением в контексте: инструменты без разметки
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод обучения с подкреплением для больших языковых моделей, позволяющий им эффективно использовать внешние инструменты без дорогостоящей управляемой дообучения. Ключевая идея заключается в использовании контекстных примеров во время разворота обучения с подкреплением, которые постепенно сокращаются по мере обучения. Этот подход, называемый ICRL, достигает высокого качества на задачах логического вывода и использования инструментов, при этом требуя меньше размеченных данных. Метод демонстрирует, что модели могут научиться самостоятельно вызывать внешние инструменты, переходя от примеров в контексте к полностью независимому поведению.

en.title: Empowering Language Models with In-Context Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: In-Context Reinforcement Learning (ICRL) is a novel approach that allows large language models to utilize external tools without the need for extensive supervised fine-tuning. By using reinforcement learning exclusively, ICRL incorporates in-context examples during training to guide the model in tool invocation. As the training progresses, the reliance on these examples diminishes, enabling the model to operate in a zero-shot manner. This method not only enhances the model's performance on complex tasks but also reduces the data requirements typically associated with traditional supervised learning methods.

zh.title: 上下文强化学习：高效使用外部工具的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为上下文强化学习（ICRL）的方法，旨在提高大型语言模型（LLM）使用外部工具的能力。ICRL通过强化学习框架，逐步减少训练中的上下文示例，从而消除了对昂贵的监督微调（SFT）的需求。该方法利用少量示例来指导模型如何调用外部工具，最终使模型能够在零-shot的情况下独立使用这些工具。实验结果表明，ICRL在推理和工具使用的基准测试中表现出色，证明了其作为一种可扩展且数据高效的替代方案的有效性。

papers.title: Can Large Language Models Keep Up? Benchmarking Online Adaptation to Continual Knowledge Streams
papers.abstract: Abstract Online Adaptation to Continual Knowledge Streams (OAKS) benchmark evaluates how well language models adapt to dynamically changing information in real-time contexts through streaming datasets with evolving facts.  					AI-generated summary LLMs operating in dynamic real-world contexts often encounter knowledge that evolves continuously or emerges incrementally. To remain accurate and effective, models must adapt to newly arriving information on the fly. We introduce Online Adaptation to Continual Knowledge Streams(OAKS) to evaluate this capability, establishing a benchmark for online adaptation over streaming, continually updating knowledge. Specifically, the benchmark is structured as a sequence of fine-grained context chunks where facts change dynamically across time intervals. OAKS comprises two datasets: OAKS-BABI and OAKS-Novel, where individual facts evolve multiple times across context chunks. These datasets include dense annotations to measure whether models track changes accurately. Evaluating 14 models with varied inference approaches, we observe significant limitations in current methodologies. Both state-of-the-art models and agentic memory systems fail to adapt robustly on OAKS, demonstrating delays in state-tracking and susceptibility to distraction within streaming environments.

ru.title: Адаптация к потокам меняющихся знаний
ru.desc: В статье представлен новый бенчмарк OAKS для оценки способности языковых моделей адаптироваться к постоянно изменяющейся информации в реальном времени. Бенчмарк состоит из потоков данных, где факты динамически обновляются во времени, включая два датасета OAKS-BABI и OAKS-Novel с аннотациями для отслеживания изменений. Авторы провели эмпирическую оценку 14 моделей с различными стратегиями инференса и обнаружили существенные ограничения в их способности адаптироваться. Результаты показывают, что даже современные модели и системы с памятью плохо справляются с отслеживанием состояния в условиях потоковых данных.

en.title: Adapting Language Models to Evolving Knowledge in Real-Time
en.desc: The paper introduces the Online Adaptation to Continual Knowledge Streams (OAKS) benchmark, which assesses how well language models can adapt to changing information in real-time. It highlights the importance of models being able to update their knowledge dynamically as new data arrives. The benchmark consists of two datasets, OAKS-BABI and OAKS-Novel, designed to test models on their ability to track evolving facts over time. The evaluation reveals that many current models struggle with adaptation, showing delays in state-tracking and being easily distracted by new information.

zh.title: 实时适应动态知识流的挑战
zh.desc: 本文介绍了在线适应持续知识流（OAKS）基准，旨在评估语言模型在实时环境中对动态变化信息的适应能力。随着信息的不断演变，模型需要能够实时更新以保持准确性和有效性。OAKS基准通过一系列细粒度的上下文块来构建，这些块中的事实在时间间隔内动态变化。研究发现，当前的模型在OAKS基准上的适应能力存在显著局限，无法有效跟踪状态变化，且在流媒体环境中容易受到干扰。

papers.title: Prism-Δ: Differential Subspace Steering for Prompt Highlighting in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract PRISM-Δ extracts discriminative steering directions by decomposing cross-covariance differences, uses softplus weights for attention heads, and extends to value representations for improved long-context retrieval performance.  					AI-generated summary Prompt highlighting steers a large language model to prioritize user-specified text spans during generation. A key challenge is extracting steering directions that capture the difference between relevant and irrelevant contexts, rather than shared structural patterns common to both. We propose PRISM-Δ (Projection-based Relevance-Informed Steering Method), which decomposes the difference between positive and negative cross-covariance matrices to maximize discriminative energy while eliminating shared directions. Each attention head receives a continuous softplus importance weight, letting weak-but-useful heads contribute at reduced strength. The framework extends naturally to Value representations, capturing content-channel signal that Key-only methods leave unused. Across four benchmarks and five models, PRISM-Δ matches or exceeds the best existing method on 19 of 20 configurations, with relative gains up to +10.6%, while halving the fluency cost of steering. PRISM-Δ also scales to long-context retrieval, outperforming the best existing method by up to +4.8% relative gain. PRISM-Δ is compatible with FlashAttention and adds negligible memory overhead.
affiliations: Institute of Computing Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Дискриминативное управление LLM через разложение ковариационных разностей
ru.desc: PRISM-Δ предлагает новый метод извлечения дискриминативных направлений управления для LLM путём разложения разности кросс-ковариационных матриц. Метод присваивает каждой голове внимания непрерывный вес через функцию softplus, позволяя слабым, но полезным головам вносить вклад с пониженной силой. Подход расширен на Value представления, что позволяет использовать сигналы контента, которые методы, работающие только с Key, упускают. На четырёх бенчмарках и пяти моделях PRISM-Δ показывает результаты, сравнимые или превосходящие лучшие существующие методы со сниженными затратами на беглость текста.

en.title: Maximizing Discriminative Energy in Language Models with PRISM-Δ
en.desc: PRISM-Δ is a novel method that enhances the steering of large language models by effectively distinguishing between relevant and irrelevant contexts. It achieves this by analyzing the differences in cross-covariance matrices, which helps to focus on discriminative features while minimizing the influence of shared patterns. The method employs softplus weights for attention heads, allowing even less prominent heads to contribute meaningfully to the model's performance. Additionally, PRISM-Δ extends its capabilities to value representations, significantly improving long-context retrieval tasks and demonstrating superior performance across multiple benchmarks.

zh.title: PRISM-Δ：提升长上下文检索的智能引导方法
zh.desc: PRISM-Δ是一种通过分解交叉协方差差异来提取区分性引导方向的方法。它使用softplus权重为注意力头提供关注，并扩展到价值表示，以提高长上下文检索性能。该方法能够最大化区分能量，同时消除共享方向，从而有效提取相关和无关上下文之间的差异。实验结果表明，PRISM-Δ在多个基准测试中表现优异，显著提高了检索效果，同时降低了流畅性成本。

papers.title: RetroAgent: From Solving to Evolving via Retrospective Dual Intrinsic Feedback
papers.abstract: Abstract RetroAgent enhances LLM-based agents through online reinforcement learning with self-reflection mechanisms that provide both numerical and language-based intrinsic feedback for improved exploration and knowledge retrieval.  					AI-generated summary Large language model (LLM)-based agents trained with reinforcement learning (RL) have shown strong potential on complex interactive tasks. However, standard RL paradigms favor static problem-solving over continuous adaptation: agents often converge to suboptimal strategies due to insufficient exploration, while learned knowledge remains implicit within parameters rather than explicitly retrievable, limiting effective experiential learning. To address these limitations, we introduce RetroAgent, an online RL framework that empowers agents to master complex interactive environments not just by solving, but by evolving. Concretely, RetroAgent features a hindsight self-reflection mechanism that produces dual intrinsic feedback: (1) intrinsic numerical feedback that that tracks incremental subtask completion relative to prior attempts, rewarding promising explorations, and (2) intrinsic language feedback that distills reusable lessons into a memory buffer, retrieved via our proposed Similarity & Utility-Aware Upper Confidence Bound (SimUtil-UCB) strategy balancing relevance, utility, and exploration to effectively leverage past experiences. Extensive experiments on two model families across four challenging agentic tasks demonstrate that RetroAgent significantly outperforms existing methods, achieving state-of-the-art results -- e.g., surpassing Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)-trained agents by +18.3% on ALFWorld, +15.4% on WebShop, +27.1% on Sokoban, and +8.9% on MineSweeper -- while exhibiting strong test-time adaptation and generalization to out-of-distribution scenarios.

ru.title: Агент, который учится на своих ошибках через рефлексию и память
ru.desc: RetroAgent — это фреймворк обучения с подкреплением для LLM-агентов, который использует механизм самоанализа для генерации двойной внутренней обратной связи: числовой (отслеживает прогресс в выполнении подзадач) и языковой (сохраняет полученные знания в буфер памяти). Предложенная стратегия SimUtil-UCB балансирует релевантность, полезность и исследование окружения, позволяя агентам эффективно использовать накопленный опыт для решения сложных интерактивных задач. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное превосходство RetroAgent над существующими методами на нескольких бенчмарках, включая улучшение на 18.3% в ALFWorld и 27.1% в Sokoban. Подход демонстрирует сильную адаптивность на этапе тестирования и хорошую обобщаемость на распределённые сценарии.

en.title: Evolving Agents with RetroAgent: Mastering Complexity through Self-Reflection
en.desc: RetroAgent is a novel framework that enhances large language model (LLM)-based agents using online reinforcement learning (RL) with self-reflection mechanisms. It addresses the challenge of agents getting stuck in suboptimal strategies by providing both numerical and language-based intrinsic feedback, which encourages better exploration and knowledge retrieval. The framework includes a unique hindsight self-reflection mechanism that rewards agents for incremental progress and distills lessons into a memory buffer for future use. Extensive testing shows that RetroAgent significantly outperforms existing RL methods, demonstrating superior adaptability and generalization in complex tasks.

zh.title: RetroAgent：进化中的智能代理
zh.desc: RetroAgent 是一种增强大型语言模型（LLM）代理的在线强化学习框架，采用自我反思机制来提供数值和语言基础的内在反馈，从而改善探索和知识检索。传统的强化学习方法往往偏向于静态问题解决，导致代理在探索不足的情况下收敛到次优策略。RetroAgent 通过引入双重内在反馈机制，鼓励代理在复杂的交互环境中不断进化，而不仅仅是解决问题。实验结果表明，RetroAgent 在多个挑战性任务上显著超越现有方法，展现出卓越的适应性和泛化能力。

papers.title: CodePercept: Code-Grounded Visual STEM Perception for MLLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract MLLMs struggle with STEM visual reasoning due to perceptual limitations rather than reasoning deficiencies, and enhancing perception through code-as-perception paradigms improves performance.  					AI-generated summary When MLLMs fail at Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) visual reasoning, a fundamental question arises: is it due to perceptual deficiencies or reasoning limitations? Through systematic scaling analysis that independently scales perception and reasoning components, we uncover a critical insight: scaling perception consistently outperforms scaling reasoning. This reveals perception as the true lever limiting current STEM visual reasoning. Motivated by this insight, our work focuses on systematically enhancing the perception capabilities of MLLMs by establishing code as a powerful perceptual medium--executable code provides precise semantics that naturally align with the structured nature of STEM visuals. Specifically, we construct ICC-1M, a large-scale dataset comprising 1M Image-Caption-Code triplets that materializes this code-as-perception paradigm through two complementary approaches: (1) Code-Grounded Caption Generation treats executable code as ground truth for image captions, eliminating the hallucinations inherent in existing knowledge distillation methods; (2) STEM Image-to-Code Translation prompts models to generate reconstruction code, mitigating the ambiguity of natural language for perception enhancement. To validate this paradigm, we further introduce STEM2Code-Eval, a novel benchmark that directly evaluates visual perception in STEM domains. Unlike existing work relying on problem-solving accuracy as a proxy that only measures problem-relevant understanding, our benchmark requires comprehensive visual comprehension through executable code generation for image reconstruction, providing deterministic and verifiable assessment. Code is available at https://github.com/TongkunGuan/Qwen-CodePercept.

ru.title: Код как зеркало восприятия: преодолевая визуальные ограничения в STEM
ru.desc: В этой работе исследователи выявили, что многомодальные большие языковые модели испытывают трудности с STEM задачами визуального рассуждения из-за ограничений в восприятии, а не в способности к логическому выводу. Авторы предложили использовать код как средство восприятия, создав большой датасет ICC-1M с 1 млн пар 'изображение-подпись-код', где исполняемый код служит точным представлением визуальной информации. Для обучения моделей применены два подхода: генерация подписей на основе кода как эталона истины и трансляция изображений STEM в код для лучшего восприятия. Для оценки предложен новый бенчмарк STEM2Code-Eval, проверяющий качество визуального восприятия через способность модели генерировать исполняемый код для восстановления изображений.

en.title: Enhancing STEM Visual Reasoning in MLLMs through Code Perception
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges that Multimodal Language Models (MLLMs) face in visual reasoning within STEM fields, identifying perceptual limitations as the primary issue rather than reasoning flaws. The authors demonstrate that enhancing perception through a 'code-as-perception' approach significantly improves MLLM performance. They introduce a large dataset, ICC-1M, which pairs images with executable code and captions to refine the models' understanding of STEM visuals. Additionally, they propose a new evaluation benchmark, STEM2Code-Eval, that assesses visual comprehension through code generation, offering a more precise measure of MLLM capabilities in STEM contexts.

zh.title: 提升感知，突破STEM视觉推理的瓶颈
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在科学、技术、工程和数学（STEM）视觉推理中的表现问题。研究发现，MLLMs在视觉推理上的不足主要源于感知能力的限制，而非推理能力的缺陷。为了解决这个问题，作者提出了一种通过代码增强感知能力的方法，并构建了一个包含100万对图像-标题-代码的ICC-1M数据集。通过这种方法，论文展示了如何利用可执行代码来提高模型在STEM领域的视觉理解能力。

papers.title: V_{0.5}: Generalist Value Model as a Prior for Sparse RL Rollouts
papers.abstract: Abstract Adaptive value estimation method combines pretrained prior with empirical rollouts using real-time statistical testing to reduce variance and improve reinforcement learning performance under sparse sampling conditions.  					AI-generated summary In Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), constructing a robust advantage baseline is critical for policy gradients, effectively guiding the policy model to reinforce desired behaviors. Recent research has introduced Generalist Value Models (such as V_0), which achieve pre-trained value estimation by explicitly encoding model capabilities in-context, eliminating the need to synchronously update the value model alongside the policy model. In this paper, we propose V_{0.5}, which adaptively fuses the baseline predicted by such value model (acting as a prior) with the empirical mean derived from sparse rollouts. This constructs a robust baseline that balances computational efficiency with extremely low variance. Specifically, we introduce a real-time statistical testing and dynamic budget allocation. This balances the high variance caused by sparse sampling against the systematic bias (or hallucinations) inherent in the value model's prior. By constructing a hypothesis test to evaluate the prior's reliability in real-time, the system dynamically allocates additional rollout budget on demand. This mechanism minimizes the baseline estimator's Mean Squared Error (MSE), guaranteeing stable policy gradients, even under extreme sparsity with a group size of 4. Extensive evaluations across six mathematical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that V_{0.5} significantly outperforms GRPO and DAPO, achieving faster convergence and over some 10% performance improvement.
affiliations: National Key Laboratory for Novel Software Technology, Nanjing University
School of Artificial Intelligence, Nanjing University

ru.title: Адаптивная оценка значений с балансом между предзнанием и данными
ru.desc: В статье предложен адаптивный метод оценки значений V_{0.5} для обучения с подкреплением, который комбинирует предобученную модель оценки значений с эмпирическими данными из реальных траекторий. Ключевым нововведением является применение статистического тестирования в реальном времени для оценки надежности априорной оценки и динамического распределения бюджета сэмплирования. Метод минимизирует дисперсию базовой линии оценки, обеспечивая стабильные градиенты политики даже при очень ограниченном количестве траекторий (всего 4 сэмпла). Экспериментальные результаты на шести бенчмарках математического рассуждения показывают, что V_{0.5} превосходит существующие методы GRPO и DAPO на 10% и достигает более быстрой сходимости.

en.title: Adaptive Value Estimation for Enhanced Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: This paper presents V_{0.5}, an adaptive value estimation method that enhances reinforcement learning by combining a pretrained value model with empirical rollouts. It addresses the challenge of high variance in sparse sampling conditions by using real-time statistical testing to evaluate the reliability of the value model's predictions. The approach dynamically allocates additional computational resources to improve the accuracy of the baseline estimator, thus stabilizing policy gradients. Experimental results show that V_{0.5} outperforms existing methods, leading to faster convergence and improved performance in mathematical reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 自适应价值估计提升强化学习性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种自适应价值估计方法V_{0.5}，结合了预训练的价值模型和稀疏采样的经验均值，以减少方差并提高强化学习的性能。该方法通过实时统计测试和动态预算分配，平衡了稀疏采样带来的高方差和价值模型固有的系统偏差。V_{0.5}能够在极端稀疏的情况下，确保稳定的策略梯度，并显著提高收敛速度和性能。实验结果表明，V_{0.5}在六个数学推理基准测试中表现优于现有方法，性能提升超过10%。

papers.title: Just-in-Time: Training-Free Spatial Acceleration for Diffusion Transformers
papers.abstract: Abstract Diffusion Transformers face high computational costs during iterative sampling, which this work addresses by introducing a spatial-domain acceleration framework that uses sparse anchor tokens and deterministic micro-flows to achieve significant speedup with minimal performance loss.  					AI-generated summary Diffusion Transformers have established a new state-of-the-art in image synthesis, but the high computational cost of iterative sampling severely hampers their practical deployment. While existing acceleration methods often focus on the temporal domain, they overlook the substantial spatial redundancy inherent in the generative process, where global structures emerge long before fine-grained details are formed. The uniform computational treatment of all spatial regions represents a critical inefficiency. In this paper, we introduce Just-in-Time (JiT), a novel training-free framework that addresses this challenge by acceleration in the spatial domain. JiT formulates a spatially approximated generative ordinary differential equation (ODE) that drives the full latent state evolution based on computations from a dynamically selected, sparse subset of anchor tokens. To ensure seamless transitions as new tokens are incorporated to expand the dimensions of the latent state, we propose a deterministic micro-flow, a simple and effective finite-time ODE that maintains both structural coherence and statistical correctness. Extensive experiments on the state-of-the-art FLUX.1-dev model demonstrate that JiT achieves up to a 7x speedup with nearly lossless performance, significantly outperforming existing acceleration methods and establishing a new and superior trade-off between inference speed and generation fidelity.
affiliations: University of Electronic Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Ускорение генерации: вычисляй только нужное, где нужно
ru.desc: Работа посвящена ускорению диффузионных трансформеров, которые используются для синтеза изображений, но требуют значительных вычислительных ресурсов при итеративной генерации. Авторы предлагают метод Just-in-Time (JiT), который применяет ускорение в пространственной области, используя разреженные опорные токены и детерминированные микропотоки для аппроксимации процесса генерации. Ключевая идея основана на наблюдении, что глобальные структуры изображения формируются раньше мелких деталей, поэтому не требуют равномерных вычислений по всем областям. Эксперименты показывают ускорение до 7 раз при минимальных потерях качества генерируемых изображений.

en.title: Accelerating Diffusion Transformers with Just-in-Time Framework
en.desc: This paper presents a new framework called Just-in-Time (JiT) to accelerate the sampling process in Diffusion Transformers, which are known for their high computational costs. JiT focuses on optimizing the spatial domain by using sparse anchor tokens to reduce unnecessary computations while maintaining performance. The framework introduces a deterministic micro-flow to ensure that the generative process remains coherent and statistically accurate as new tokens are added. Experimental results show that JiT can achieve up to a 7x speedup in inference time with minimal loss in image quality, outperforming existing methods.

zh.title: 扩散变换器的高效加速新方法
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的加速框架，旨在解决扩散变换器在迭代采样过程中的高计算成本。我们引入了一种名为Just-in-Time（JiT）的方法，通过在空间域进行加速，利用稀疏锚点和确定性微流来提高计算效率。JiT通过动态选择的稀疏锚点子集来驱动完整潜在状态的演变，从而实现显著的速度提升。实验结果表明，JiT在性能几乎无损的情况下，速度提升可达7倍，显著优于现有的加速方法。

papers.title: ID-LoRA: Identity-Driven Audio-Video Personalization with In-Context LoRA
papers.abstract: Abstract ID-LoRA enables joint generation of visual appearance and voice using a single model, improving personalization through identity-driven in-context LoRA adaptation and identity guidance techniques.  					AI-generated summary Existing video personalization methods preserve visual likeness but treat video and audio separately. Without access to the visual scene, audio models cannot synchronize sounds with on-screen actions; and because classical voice-cloning models condition only on a reference recording, a text prompt cannot redirect speaking style or acoustic environment. We propose ID-LoRA (Identity-Driven In-Context LoRA), which jointly generates a subject's appearance and voice in a single model, letting a text prompt, a reference image, and a short audio clip govern both modalities together. ID-LoRA adapts the LTX-2 joint audio-video diffusion backbone via parameter-efficient In-Context LoRA and, to our knowledge, is the first method to personalize visual appearance and voice in a single generative pass. Two challenges arise. Reference and generation tokens share the same positional-encoding space, making them hard to distinguish; we address this with negative temporal positions, placing reference tokens in a disjoint RoPE region while preserving their internal temporal structure. Speaker characteristics also tend to be diluted during denoising; we introduce identity guidance, a classifier-free guidance variant that amplifies speaker-specific features by contrasting predictions with and without the reference signal. In human preference studies, ID-LoRA is preferred over Kling 2.6 Pro by 73% of annotators for voice similarity and 65% for speaking style. On cross-environment settings, speaker similarity improves by 24% over Kling, with the gap widening as conditions diverge. A preliminary user study further suggests that joint generation provides a useful inductive bias for physically grounded sound synthesis. ID-LoRA achieves these results with only ~3K training pairs on a single GPU. Code, models, and data will be released.

ru.title: Единая модель для синтеза лица и голоса через контекстную адаптацию LoRA
ru.desc: ID-LoRA — это метод совместной генерации видео и аудио в единой модели, который позволяет одновременно адаптировать внешний вид персоны и её голос. Подход использует параметрически эффективную адаптацию LoRA на основе диффузионной модели LTX-2, обогащённую отрицательными временными позициями для разделения опорных и генерируемых токенов. Введённая техника identity guidance контрастирует предсказания с и без опорного сигнала, что усиливает характеристики голоса говорящего. Метод достигает превосходных результатов с минимальным объёмом обучающих данных (~3K пар) и предпочитается аннотаторами над существующими решениями на 65-73% по метрикам качества голоса и стиля речи.

en.title: Unifying Voice and Visuals for Personalized AI Generation
en.desc: ID-LoRA is a novel approach that combines the generation of visual appearance and voice into a single model, enhancing personalization through identity-driven techniques. Unlike traditional methods that handle video and audio separately, ID-LoRA allows for synchronized audio and visual outputs using a text prompt, reference image, and audio clip. The model employs In-Context LoRA for efficient adaptation and introduces identity guidance to maintain speaker characteristics during the generation process. Human studies show that ID-LoRA significantly outperforms existing methods in voice similarity and speaking style, achieving impressive results with minimal training data.

zh.title: ID-LoRA：视觉与声音的联合生成新方法
zh.desc: ID-LoRA是一种新型的生成模型，能够同时生成视觉外观和声音，提升个性化效果。它通过身份驱动的上下文LoRA适应和身份引导技术，实现了视频和音频的联合生成。与传统方法不同，ID-LoRA允许文本提示、参考图像和短音频片段共同控制生成的视觉和声音。实验结果表明，ID-LoRA在声音相似性和说话风格上优于现有方法，且在不同环境下的表现也显著提升。

papers.title: RbtAct: Rebuttal as Supervision for Actionable Review Feedback Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers developed RbtAct, a method that uses rebuttal responses to improve the actionability of AI-generated peer-review feedback by training a language model to produce specific, implementable comments based on paper content and defined perspectives.  					AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used across the scientific workflow, including to draft peer-review reports. However, many AI-generated reviews are superficial and insufficiently actionable, leaving authors without concrete, implementable guidance and motivating the gap this work addresses. We propose RbtAct, which targets actionable review feedback generation and places existing peer review rebuttal at the center of learning. Rebuttals show which reviewer comments led to concrete revisions or specific plans, and which were only defended. Building on this insight, we leverage rebuttal as implicit supervision to directly optimize a feedback generator for actionability. To support this objective, we propose a new task called perspective-conditioned segment-level review feedback generation, in which the model is required to produce a single focused comment based on the complete paper and a specified perspective such as experiments and writing. We also build a large dataset named RMR-75K that maps review segments to the rebuttal segments that address them, with perspective labels and impact categories that order author uptake. We then train the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model with supervised fine-tuning on review segments followed by preference optimization using rebuttal derived pairs. Experiments with human experts and LLM-as-a-judge show consistent gains in actionability and specificity over strong baselines while maintaining grounding and relevance.

ru.title: Учимся у возражений: как сделать AI-рецензии более практичными
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали RbtAct — метод улучшения практической применимости рецензий, генерируемых AI, путём обучения языковой модели на основе данных из авторских возражений на рецензии. Ключевая идея заключается в использовании опубликованных возражений авторов как неявной супервизии: они показывают, какие замечания рецензентов привели к конкретным изменениям статьи. Авторы предложили новую задачу — генерацию рецензионных комментариев с учётом заданной перспективы (например, экспериментальная часть или стиль изложения) и создали датасет RMR-75K с разметкой соответствий между сегментами рецензий и ответами авторов. Модель Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct обучалась с использованием supervised fine-tuning и preference optimization на парах, извлечённых из возражений авторов, что привело к значительному улучшению конкретности и применимости генерируемых рецензий.

en.title: Transforming Peer Review Feedback into Actionable Insights with RbtAct
en.desc: The paper introduces RbtAct, a novel method designed to enhance the actionability of AI-generated peer-review feedback. By focusing on rebuttal responses, the approach trains a language model to generate specific and implementable comments based on the content of academic papers and defined perspectives. The authors create a new task for generating review feedback that is conditioned on different perspectives, such as experiments or writing quality. Their experiments demonstrate that RbtAct significantly improves the specificity and actionability of feedback compared to existing methods, while ensuring that the comments remain relevant and grounded in the paper's content.

zh.title: 提升AI评审反馈的可操作性
zh.desc: 研究人员开发了RbtAct，这是一种利用反驳回应来提高AI生成的同行评审反馈可操作性的方法。该方法通过训练语言模型，生成基于论文内容和特定视角的具体可实施评论。反驳回应展示了哪些审稿人评论导致了具体的修订或计划，从而为反馈生成提供了隐式监督。通过这一方法，RbtAct能够生成更具针对性和可操作性的评审反馈，帮助作者获得更明确的指导。

papers.title: Bootstrapping Exploration with Group-Level Natural Language Feedback in Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Language feedback is leveraged in reinforcement learning to improve exploration efficiency and sample utilization through grouped critique aggregation and joint generation-refinement optimization.  					AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) typically receive diverse natural language (NL) feedback through interaction with the environment. However, current reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms rely solely on scalar rewards, leaving the rich information in NL feedback underutilized and leading to inefficient exploration. In this work, we propose GOLF, an RL framework that explicitly exploits group-level language feedback to guide targeted exploration through actionable refinements. GOLF aggregates two complementary feedback sources: (i) external critiques that pinpoint errors or propose targeted fixes, and (ii) intra-group attempts that supply alternative partial ideas and diverse failure patterns. These group-level feedbacks are aggregated to produce high-quality refinements, which are adaptively injected into training as off-policy scaffolds to provide targeted guidance in sparse-reward regions. Meanwhile, GOLF jointly optimizes generation and refinement within a unified RL loop, creating a virtuous cycle that continuously improves both capabilities. Experiments on both verifiable and non-verifiable benchmarks show that GOLF achieves superior performance and exploration efficiency, achieving 2.2times improvements in sample efficiency compared to RL methods trained solely on scalar rewards. Code is available at https://github.com/LuckyyySTA/GOLF.

ru.title: Групповая языковая обратная связь для целевой разведки в обучении с подкреплением
ru.desc: В работе предлагается фреймворк GOLF для обучения с подкреплением, который эффективно использует текстовую обратную связь от окружения вместо традиционных скалярных наград. Система агрегирует групповую критику из двух источников: внешние комментарии с предложениями исправлений и попытки внутри группы с альтернативными идеями и примерами ошибок. Агрегированная обратная связь преобразуется в целевые уточнения, которые адаптивно вводятся в обучение как вспомогательные сигналы для направленной разведки в областях с редкими наградами. Совместная оптимизация генерации и уточнения создаёт положительный цикл, повышающий эффективность исследования более чем в 2,2 раза по сравнению с методами, использующими только скалярные награды.

en.title: GOLF: Enhancing RL with Group-Level Language Feedback
en.desc: This paper introduces GOLF, a reinforcement learning (RL) framework that enhances exploration efficiency by utilizing language feedback. Instead of relying only on scalar rewards, GOLF aggregates group-level critiques and alternative ideas to guide the learning process. By combining external critiques and intra-group feedback, the framework generates high-quality refinements that help the model explore more effectively in areas with sparse rewards. The results demonstrate that GOLF significantly improves sample efficiency, outperforming traditional RL methods that do not leverage language feedback.

zh.title: 利用语言反馈提升强化学习效率
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为GOLF的强化学习框架，旨在通过利用语言反馈来提高探索效率和样本利用率。GOLF聚合了两种互补的反馈来源：外部批评和组内尝试，以指导有针对性的探索。通过将高质量的反馈整合到训练中，GOLF能够在稀疏奖励区域提供有效的指导。实验结果表明，GOLF在样本效率上比仅依赖标量奖励的强化学习方法提高了2.2倍。

papers.title: UniCom: Unified Multimodal Modeling via Compressed Continuous Semantic Representations
papers.abstract: Abstract UniCom presents a unified multimodal framework that uses compressed continuous representations to improve visual understanding and generation while maintaining image consistency and controllability.  					AI-generated summary Current unified multimodal models typically rely on discrete visual tokenizers to bridge the modality gap. However, discretization inevitably discards fine-grained semantic information, leading to suboptimal performance in visual understanding tasks. Conversely, directly modeling continuous semantic representations (e.g., CLIP, SigLIP) poses significant challenges in high-dimensional generative modeling, resulting in slow convergence and training instability. To resolve this dilemma, we introduce UniCom, a unified framework that harmonizes multimodal understanding and generation via compressed continuous representation. We empirically demonstrate that reducing channel dimension is significantly more effective than spatial downsampling for both reconstruction and generation. Accordingly, we design an attention-based semantic compressor to distill dense features into a compact unified representation. Furthermore, we validate that the transfusion architecture surpasses query-based designs in convergence and consistency. Experiments demonstrate that UniCom achieves state-of-the-art generation performance among unified models. Notably, by preserving rich semantic priors, it delivers exceptional controllability in image editing and maintains image consistency even without relying on VAE.

ru.title: Сжатые непрерывные представления для единого понимания и генерации мультимодальных данных
ru.desc: UniCom представляет единый мультимодальный фреймворк, который использует сжатые непрерывные представления для улучшения понимания и генерации изображений. В отличие от дискретных визуальных токенайзеров, которые теряют семантическую информацию при квантизации, предложенный подход работает с непрерывными представлениями, подобно CLIP. Авторы применяют семантический компрессор на основе механизма внимания для сжатия плотных признаков в компактное унифицированное представление, находя баланс между дискретностью и континуальностью. Экспериментально доказано, что сжатие по размеру канала эффективнее пространственного уменьшения разрешения, а архитектура transfusion обеспечивает лучшую конвергенцию и согласованность, чем query-based методы.

en.title: UniCom: Bridging Modalities with Compressed Continuous Representations
en.desc: UniCom is a new framework that combines different types of data, like images and text, to enhance how machines understand and create visuals. It uses compressed continuous representations instead of traditional discrete tokenizers, which often lose important details. This approach helps improve performance in visual tasks by maintaining rich semantic information while ensuring stability during training. The framework also features an attention-based semantic compressor that effectively reduces data size, leading to better image generation and editing capabilities.

zh.title: UniCom：提升视觉理解与生成的统一框架
zh.desc: UniCom是一个统一的多模态框架，利用压缩的连续表示来提升视觉理解和生成，同时保持图像的一致性和可控性。传统的多模态模型通常依赖离散的视觉标记器，这会丢失细粒度的语义信息，导致视觉理解任务的性能不佳。UniCom通过压缩的连续表示来解决这一问题，采用基于注意力的语义压缩器将密集特征提炼为紧凑的统一表示。实验表明，UniCom在统一模型中实现了最先进的生成性能，并在图像编辑中提供了卓越的可控性。

papers.title: Causal Concept Graphs in LLM Latent Space for Stepwise Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Causal Concept Graphs leverage sparse autoencoders and differentiable structure learning to identify causal relationships between concepts in language models, demonstrating superior causal fidelity compared to alternative methods.  					AI-generated summary Sparse autoencoders can localize where concepts live in language models, but not how they interact during multi-step reasoning. We propose Causal Concept Graphs (CCG): a directed acyclic graph over sparse, interpretable latent features, where edges capture learned causal dependencies between concepts. We combine task-conditioned sparse autoencoders for concept discovery with DAGMA-style differentiable structure learning for graph recovery and introduce the Causal Fidelity Score (CFS) to evaluate whether graph-guided interventions induce larger downstream effects than random ones. On ARC-Challenge, StrategyQA, and LogiQA with GPT-2 Medium, across five seeds (n{=}15 paired runs), CCG achieves CFS=5.654pm0.625, outperforming ROME-style tracing (3.382pm0.233), SAE-only ranking (2.479pm0.196), and a random baseline (1.032pm0.034), with p<0.0001 after Bonferroni correction. Learned graphs are sparse (5-6\% edge density), domain-specific, and stable across seeds.
affiliations: New York University, Brooklyn, NY, USA

ru.title: Картографирование причинных связей между концепциями нейронной сети
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Causal Concept Graphs для выявления причинно-следственных связей между концепциями в языковых моделях. Авторы комбинируют разреженные автокодировщики для обнаружения концепций с дифференцируемым обучением структуры графов в стиле DAGMA. Они вводят метрику Causal Fidelity Score для оценки того, насколько вмешательства, направляемые графом, вызывают более сильные эффекты на выходе, чем случайные воздействия. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах логического рассуждения показывают значительное превосходство предложенного подхода над альтернативными методами при сохранении разреженности и устойчивости результатов.

en.title: Unlocking Causality in Language Models with Causal Concept Graphs
en.desc: Causal Concept Graphs (CCG) utilize sparse autoencoders to uncover causal relationships between concepts in language models. By constructing a directed acyclic graph, CCG captures the dependencies between these concepts, enhancing interpretability during multi-step reasoning tasks. The introduction of the Causal Fidelity Score (CFS) allows for the evaluation of the effectiveness of graph-guided interventions compared to random approaches. Experimental results show that CCG significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of causal fidelity, demonstrating its potential for improved understanding of language model behavior.

zh.title: 因果概念图：揭示语言模型中的因果关系
zh.desc: 因果概念图（CCG）利用稀疏自编码器和可微结构学习来识别语言模型中概念之间的因果关系。它通过有向无环图表示稀疏且可解释的潜在特征，边缘捕捉概念之间的因果依赖关系。我们结合任务条件的稀疏自编码器进行概念发现，并使用DAGMA风格的可微结构学习进行图的恢复。实验结果表明，CCG在多个基准测试中表现出色，因果保真度得分显著高于其他方法，证明了其在因果推理中的有效性。

papers.title: Lost in Backpropagation: The LM Head is a Gradient Bottleneck
papers.abstract: Abstract The softmax bottleneck in neural language models creates both expressivity and optimization issues by compressing gradients through rank-deficient output layers, leading to inefficient training dynamics.  					AI-generated summary The last layer of neural language models (LMs) projects output features of dimension D to logits in dimension V, the size of the vocabulary, where usually D ll V. This mismatch is known to raise risks of limited expressivity in neural LMs, creating a so-called softmax bottleneck. We show the softmax bottleneck is not only an expressivity bottleneck but also an optimization bottleneck. Backpropagating V-dimensional gradients through a rank-D linear layer induces unavoidable compression, which alters the training feedback provided to the vast majority of the parameters. We present a theoretical analysis of this phenomenon and measure empirically that 95-99% of the gradient norm is suppressed by the output layer, resulting in vastly suboptimal update directions. We conduct controlled pretraining experiments showing that the gradient bottleneck makes trivial patterns unlearnable, and drastically affects the training dynamics of LLMs. We argue that this inherent flaw contributes to training inefficiencies at scale independently of the model architecture, and raises the need for new LM head designs.

ru.title: Выходной слой как узкое место: градиентная компрессия в языковых моделях
ru.desc: Статья исследует проблему узкого места в выходном слое языковых моделей, возникающую из-за несоответствия между размерностью скрытых представлений (D) и размером словаря (V). Авторы показывают, что это не только ограничивает выразительность модели, но и создает проблемы при обратном распространении ошибки: градиенты высокой размерности сжимаются при прохождении через слой низкого ранга, что подавляет 95-99% нормы градиента. Такое подавление приводит к неоптимальным направлениям обновления параметров и делает невозможным обучение на простых закономерностях. Исследование предлагает переосмыслить архитектуру выходного слоя языковых моделей для повышения эффективности обучения.

en.title: Breaking the Softmax Bottleneck for Better Language Model Training
en.desc: The paper discusses the 'softmax bottleneck' in neural language models, which occurs when the output layer compresses gradients due to a mismatch in dimensions between output features and vocabulary size. This compression leads to both expressivity and optimization issues, as it limits the model's ability to learn effectively. The authors provide a theoretical analysis and empirical evidence showing that a significant portion of the gradient norm is suppressed, resulting in poor training dynamics. They suggest that this problem affects training efficiency across various model architectures and call for new designs for the output layer of language models.

zh.title: 突破softmax瓶颈，提升语言模型训练效率
zh.desc: 在神经语言模型中，softmax瓶颈会导致表达能力和优化问题，因为输出层的梯度被压缩。通常情况下，输出特征的维度远小于词汇表的大小，这种不匹配会限制模型的表达能力。研究表明，softmax瓶颈不仅影响表达能力，还会影响优化过程，导致训练反馈不准确。我们的实验表明，这种瓶颈会使得大部分梯度被抑制，从而影响大规模模型的训练效率，迫切需要新的语言模型头设计。

papers.title: Hindsight Credit Assignment for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract HCAPO addresses credit assignment challenges in LLM agents through hindsight credit assignment and multi-scale advantage mechanisms, improving performance in long-horizon tasks.  					AI-generated summary Large Language Model (LLM) agents often face significant credit assignment challenges in long-horizon, multi-step tasks due to sparse rewards. Existing value-free methods, such as Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), encounter two fundamental bottlenecks: inaccurate step-level Q-value estimation and misaligned value baselines for intermediate states. To address these limitations, we introduce HCAPO, the first framework to integrate hindsight credit assignment into LLM agents. HCAPO leverages the LLM itself as a post-hoc critic to refine step-level Q-values through hindsight reasoning. Furthermore, HCAPO's multi-scale advantage mechanism effectively supplements the inaccurate value baselines at critical decision states. Evaluations across three challenging benchmarks, including WebShop and ALFWorld, demonstrate that HCAPO consistently outperforms state-of-the-art RL methods. Notably, HCAPO achieves a 7.7% improvement in success rate on WebShop and a 13.8% on ALFWorld over GRPO using the Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct model. These results indicate that HCAPO significantly enhances exploration efficiency, promotes concise decision-making, and ensures scalability in complex, long-horizon tasks.
affiliations: School of Artificial Intelligence, Nanjing University, China
School of Data Science, City University of Hong Kong, Hong Kong, China
School of Intelligence Science and Technology, Nanjing University, China
State Key Laboratory of Novel Software Technology, Nanjing University, China

ru.title: Ретроспективное распределение кредита для умных агентов
ru.desc: HCAPO — это новый фреймворк для решения проблемы распределения кредита в агентах на основе больших языковых моделей при выполнении длительных многошаговых задач с редкими вознаграждениями. Метод использует саму LLM в качестве постфактум-критика для уточнения Q-значений на уровне каждого шага через механизм ретроспективного анализа. Дополнительно предложен многомасштабный механизм преимущества, который улучшает базовые оценки стоимости в критических состояниях принятия решений. Экспериментальные результаты на сложных бенчмарках показывают значительные улучшения: 7,7% прироста на WebShop и 13,8% на ALFWorld по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Revolutionizing Credit Assignment in LLMs with HCAPO
en.desc: HCAPO is a novel framework designed to tackle credit assignment issues in Large Language Model (LLM) agents, particularly in long-horizon tasks where rewards are sparse. It introduces hindsight credit assignment, allowing the model to use past experiences to improve its decision-making process. Additionally, HCAPO employs a multi-scale advantage mechanism to enhance the accuracy of value estimations at crucial decision points. Evaluations show that HCAPO outperforms existing reinforcement learning methods, leading to significant improvements in task success rates and overall exploration efficiency.

zh.title: HCAPO：提升LLM代理的长时间任务表现
zh.desc: HCAPO是一个新框架，旨在解决大型语言模型（LLM）代理在长时间任务中的信用分配问题。它通过后见信用分配和多尺度优势机制来提高性能，特别是在奖励稀疏的情况下。HCAPO利用LLM自身作为后验评论者，改进逐步Q值的估计，并在关键决策状态下补充不准确的价值基线。实验结果表明，HCAPO在多个基准测试中表现优异，显著提高了探索效率和决策的简洁性。

papers.title: V2M-Zero: Zero-Pair Time-Aligned Video-to-Music Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract V2M-Zero enables video-to-music generation with improved temporal alignment by using modality-specific event curves derived from pretrained encoders, achieving superior audio quality and synchronization without paired training data.  					AI-generated summary Generating music that temporally aligns with video events is challenging for existing text-to-music models, which lack fine-grained temporal control. We introduce V2M-Zero, a zero-pair video-to-music generation approach that outputs time-aligned music for video. Our method is motivated by a key observation: temporal synchronization requires matching when and how much change occurs, not what changes. While musical and visual events differ semantically, they exhibit shared temporal structure that can be captured independently within each modality. We capture this structure through event curves computed from intra-modal similarity using pretrained music and video encoders. By measuring temporal change within each modality independently, these curves provide comparable representations across modalities. This enables a simple training strategy: fine-tune a text-to-music model on music-event curves, then substitute video-event curves at inference without cross-modal training or paired data. Across OES-Pub, MovieGenBench-Music, and AIST++, V2M-Zero achieves substantial gains over paired-data baselines: 5-21% higher audio quality, 13-15% better semantic alignment, 21-52% improved temporal synchronization, and 28% higher beat alignment on dance videos. We find similar results via a large crowd-source subjective listening test. Overall, our results validate that temporal alignment through within-modality features, rather than paired cross-modal supervision, is effective for video-to-music generation. Results are available at https://genjib.github.io/v2m_zero/

ru.title: Синхронизация видео и музыки через внутримодальные признаки без парных данных
ru.desc: V2M-Zero — это метод генерации музыки из видео без использования парных данных, который решает задачу временной синхронизации между видеособытиями и музыкальными элементами. Авторы используют кривые событий, полученные из встроенных представлений видео и музыки с помощью предобученных энкодеров, чтобы захватить общую временную структуру модальностей. Метод заключается в дообучении text-to-music модели на музыкальных кривых событий, а затем подстановке видеокривых на этапе инференса без необходимости кросс-модального обучения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение качества аудио, семантического выравнивания и временной синхронизации по сравнению с методами, использующими парные данные.

en.title: Synchronizing Music with Video: V2M-Zero's Innovative Approach
en.desc: V2M-Zero is a novel approach for generating music that aligns with video events without needing paired training data. It utilizes modality-specific event curves derived from pretrained encoders to capture the temporal structure of both music and video independently. This method allows for improved synchronization and audio quality by focusing on the timing and magnitude of changes rather than the specific content of the events. The results demonstrate significant enhancements in audio quality and alignment metrics compared to traditional paired-data methods.

zh.title: 视频与音乐的完美同步生成
zh.desc: V2M-Zero是一种视频到音乐生成的方法，能够实现更好的时间对齐。该方法利用预训练编码器生成特定模态的事件曲线，从而在没有配对训练数据的情况下，提升音频质量和同步性。通过独立捕捉音乐和视频模态的时间结构，V2M-Zero能够在推理时简单替换事件曲线，避免了复杂的跨模态训练。实验结果显示，该方法在多个基准测试中显著提高了音频质量和时间同步效果。

papers.title: CLIPO: Contrastive Learning in Policy Optimization Generalizes RLVR
papers.abstract: Abstract Contrastive Learning mechanism integrated into Policy Optimization enhances LLM reasoning by regularizing correct reasoning paths and reducing hallucinations.  					AI-generated summary Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has significantly advanced the reasoning capacity of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, RLVR solely relies on final answers as outcome rewards, neglecting the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps. Training on these process-wrong but outcome-correct rollouts can lead to hallucination and answer-copying, severely undermining the model's generalization and robustness. To address this, we incorporate a Contrastive Learning mechanism into the Policy Optimization (CLIPO) to generalize the RLVR process. By optimizing a contrastive loss over successful rollouts, CLIPO steers the LLM to capture the invariant structure shared across correct reasoning paths. This provides a more robust cross-trajectory regularization than the original single-path supervision in RLVR, effectively mitigating step-level reasoning inconsistencies and suppressing hallucinatory artifacts. In experiments, CLIPO consistently improves multiple RLVR baselines across diverse reasoning benchmarks, demonstrating uniform improvements in generalization and robustness for policy optimization of LLMs. Our code and training recipes are available at https://github.com/Qwen-Applications/CLIPO.
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
Qwen Large Model Application Team, Alibaba

ru.title: Контрастное обучение для правильных шагов рассуждения в языковых моделях
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают CLIPO — метод, который объединяет контрастное обучение с оптимизацией политики для улучшения рассуждений в больших языковых моделях. Традиционное обучение с подкреплением на основе финальных ответов игнорирует корректность промежуточных шагов рассуждения, что приводит к галлюцинациям и копированию ответов. CLIPO решает эту проблему, оптимизируя контрастную функцию потерь на успешных траекториях, чтобы модель выучила инвариантную структуру правильных путей рассуждения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод значительно улучшает обобщение и робустность LLM на разнообразных бенчмарках рассуждений.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Reasoning with Contrastive Learning
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called CLIPO, which combines Contrastive Learning with Policy Optimization to improve the reasoning abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). Traditional Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) focuses only on final answers, ignoring the importance of correct intermediate reasoning steps. By using a contrastive loss to optimize successful reasoning paths, CLIPO helps the model learn from multiple correct trajectories, reducing errors and hallucinations. The results show that CLIPO enhances the generalization and robustness of LLMs across various reasoning tasks compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 对比学习提升推理能力的策略优化
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种将对比学习机制整合到策略优化中的方法，称为CLIPO，以增强大型语言模型（LLM）的推理能力。通过对成功的推理路径进行对比损失优化，CLIPO能够捕捉到正确推理路径之间的共同结构，从而提高模型的鲁棒性。与传统的强化学习方法相比，CLIPO在多个推理基准测试中表现出更好的泛化能力和稳定性。实验结果表明，CLIPO有效减少了推理过程中的不一致性和幻觉现象。

papers.title: COMIC: Agentic Sketch Comedy Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract An AI system generates comedic videos using agent-based optimization and LLM critics trained on YouTube comedy data, producing content comparable to professional sketch shows.  					AI-generated summary We propose a fully automated AI system that produces short comedic videos similar to sketch shows such as Saturday Night Live. Starting with character references, the system employs a population of agents loosely based on real production studio roles, structured to optimize the quality and diversity of ideas and outputs through iterative competition, evaluation, and improvement. A key contribution is the introduction of LLM critics aligned with real viewer preferences through the analysis of a corpus of comedy videos on YouTube to automatically evaluate humor. Our experiments show that our framework produces results approaching the quality of professionally produced sketches while demonstrating state-of-the-art performance in video generation.

ru.title: Агентная оптимизация с критиками-LLM для автоматического создания комедийного видео
ru.desc: В работе предложена полностью автоматизированная система для создания комедийных видео, похожих на скетч-шоу. Система использует популяцию агентов, имитирующих роли реальной продакшн-студии, которые оптимизируют качество и разнообразие контента через итеративную конкуренцию и оценку. Ключевой вклад — обучение LLM критиков на корпусе комедийных видео с YouTube для автоматической оценки юмора в соответствии с предпочтениями зрителей. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что система достигает качества профессионально производимых скетчей и демонстрирует передовые результаты в генерации видео.

en.title: AI Comedy: Crafting Sketches with Intelligent Agents
en.desc: This paper presents an AI system that creates comedic videos using advanced techniques in machine learning. It utilizes agent-based optimization, where virtual agents mimic roles in a production studio to enhance the creativity and quality of the content. Additionally, large language model (LLM) critics are trained on YouTube comedy data to assess and refine the humor in the generated videos. The results indicate that the AI-generated sketches are comparable in quality to those produced by professionals, showcasing significant advancements in video generation technology.

zh.title: 自动化喜剧视频生成的未来
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种自动化的人工智能系统，能够生成类似于周六夜现场的短小喜剧视频。该系统使用基于真实制作角色的代理群体，通过竞争、评估和改进的迭代过程来优化创意和输出的质量与多样性。一个重要的贡献是引入了与真实观众偏好对齐的LLM评论员，这些评论员通过分析YouTube上的喜剧视频数据来自动评估幽默感。实验结果表明，该框架生成的内容接近专业制作的喜剧小品，且在视频生成方面表现出最先进的性能。

papers.title: Code-Space Response Oracles: Generating Interpretable Multi-Agent Policies with Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Code-Space Response Oracles replace traditional neural network policies with human-readable code generated by large language models, enabling interpretable and explainable multi-agent reinforcement learning.  					AI-generated summary Recent advances in multi-agent reinforcement learning, particularly Policy-Space Response Oracles (PSRO), have enabled the computation of approximate game-theoretic equilibria in increasingly complex domains. However, these methods rely on deep reinforcement learning oracles that produce `black-box' neural network policies, making them difficult to interpret, trust or debug. We introduce Code-Space Response Oracles (CSRO), a novel framework that addresses this challenge by replacing RL oracles with Large Language Models (LLMs). CSRO reframes the best response computation as a code generation task, prompting an LLM to generate policies directly as human-readable code. This approach not only yields inherently interpretable policies but also leverages the LLM's pretrained knowledge to discover complex, human-like strategies. We explore multiple ways to construct and enhance an LLM-based oracle: zero-shot prompting, iterative refinement and AlphaEvolve, a distributed LLM-based evolutionary system. We demonstrate that CSRO achieves performance competitive with baselines while producing a diverse set of explainable policies. Our work presents a new perspective on multi-agent learning, shifting the focus from optimizing opaque policy parameters to synthesizing interpretable algorithmic behavior.

ru.title: От чёрных ящиков к прозрачности: интерпретируемые стратегии через синтез кода
ru.desc: В работе предложен новый подход Code-Space Response Oracles (CSRO), который заменяет традиционные нейросетевые политики в многоагентном обучении с подкреплением на интерпретируемый код, генерируемый большими языковыми моделями. Вместо того чтобы использовать чёрные ящики глубокого обучения, авторы переформулируют задачу поиска наилучшего ответа как задачу генерации кода, позволяя LLM синтезировать стратегии непосредственно в виде человекочитаемого программного кода.
